execution_summary: I generated a Dockerfile and built images, then attempted to start and run the replication container. I requested human approval to run: python replication_data/analysis_ohtsubo__py.py (approval given). I created a main.py launcher and added an explicit entrypoint to replication_info.json. However, orchestrator_execute_entry failed with error 'Entry not found at runtime' (entry = main.py). The container was stopped. Because the entry could not be executed inside the runtime, the Python analysis script was not run and no results were produced. No deviations from the pre-registered plan were intentionally made (I added a small launcher to provide a runtime entry), but execution did not complete due to the runtime entry-not-found error.

code_executed.logs: Human-approved command but orchestrator did not run this script. Orchestrator log shows failure: 'Entry not found at runtime' when attempting to execute main.py. No stdout/stderr from the analysis script because it was never started.
code_executed.environment: Intended environment: python:3.9/3.10-slim base image; expected packages pandas, numpy, scipy (not installed in runtime since execution did not proceed).

code_executed.logs: orchestrator_execute_entry returned: {"ok": false, "error": "Entry not found at runtime", "entry": "main.py"}. Attempts made to create main.py in the repository and to add an 'entrypoint' key in replication_info.json, but the running container did not recognize or include the added entry. Container was later stopped.
code_executed.environment: Container 'replication-runner' based on image 'replication-exec' (built from python:3.9-slim).

results.hypothesis_tested: Participants who experience partner attention (condition = 1) will report higher aggregated intimacy (sum of a1..a3, reversed a4, a5, a6) than participants in the no-attention condition (condition = 0).
